arcs.what_party_knows: Strange engineered creatures have been appearing across the region. The gems found on defeated monstrosities seem connected. Someone is manufacturing these things. The trail leads to the ruins of an abandoned settlement in the southern marches.
arcs.what_actually_happened: Viktor Voss survived military imprisonment because Percival extracted him and funded an entirely new research facility beneath the ruins of Dunnshire — a city Voss himself destroyed twelve years ago. The gem in the kobold crate during the Prologue was destined for Voss's laboratory. The party has been following the breadcrumbs of his research since the first session without knowing it.
arcs.hooks_forward: The full reveal of Percival's involvement. Kaelor Thrask's realization that the kingdom's secrecy enabled this. The question of what Percival intends to do with a functional Resonance Engine — and whether the party now knows too much.
arcs.gm_notes: The encounter's mechanical core is the emotional resonance system — players' actions charge the gems, gems discharge and alter the battlefield, positive emotional resonance (inspiration, triumph, hope) turns out to be more powerful than suffering, which visibly disturbs Voss mid-combat. He should begin taking notes at some point during Phase 2. The narrative peak is not the combat — it is the moment someone in the party pieces together that the gem from the kobold crate, the creatures at the carnival, the sarcophagus in Dunmore, and the laboratory around them are all the same project.

steps.label: Vitrified streets — black glass fused into stone, armor, and windows
Skeletons frozen in defensive positions. Evacuation carts overturned.
Nothing grows. Air unnaturally still. Whispers at night.
The entrance: collapsed military tunnels, quarantine chambers, buried infrastructure. No gates. No theater.
Steam hissing through copper conduits. Mechanical arms overhead. Wet movement in the dark.
Surgical slabs. Preservation tanks. Restrained creatures breathing heavily.
First interaction with resonance gems — a crit is absorbed. Energy ripples into the conduits.
Morrow — the original cavehopper. Horrible recognition for anyone who fought kobold cavehoppers in the Prologue.
Viktor Voss emerges — calm, articulate, clinical. He introduces himself as if beginning a consultation.
Resonance Engine is mostly stable. Players begin learning emotional triggers and crit absorption.
Voss observes the party. He is not panicking. He is studying them.
Gems begin discharging. First containment failure — creatures enter combat.
The laboratory becomes unstable. Environmental hazards activate.
Voss grows increasingly fascinated. He begins taking notes mid-combat.
Morrow joins the fight — or moves to protect Voss if Inspiration resonance fires.
Triumph and Inspiration resonance destabilize the system beyond control.
Voss realizes — audibly — that positive emotional resonance is more powerful than suffering.
The engine overloads. Structural collapse begins. Creatures frenzy. Hazards escalate every round.
Voss may inject raw resonance into himself in desperation to continue observing.
The laboratory becomes a living emotional reactor. The party must escape or finish it here.
Evidence in the lab connects directly to the kobold cavehopper operation — and to the gem in the crate.
Voss's records name Percival Malden as his sponsor. The Concord never knew.
Kaelor Thrask — if the party has any connection to him — will eventually receive word and speak two words: 'Blackglass. No.'

actions: Claw Flurry: +6 to hit, two attacks 1d6+3 slashing; if both hit, target loses reactions

actions: Resonance Vomit (Recharge 5–6): 15 ft cone, DEX Save DC 14, 4d6 psychic/acid, failure: emotional triggers count double

creatures.name: Tendril Mass
creatures.subtitle: Nervous System Colony
creatures.description: A large ooze/aberration — an autonomous nervous system colony that feeds on emotional resonance. Half damage from piercing and psychic. Heals whenever nearby creatures experience fear, pain, or grief.
traits: Distributed Biology — half damage from piercing and psychic
Emotional Feeding — heals 5 HP whenever nearby creatures experience fear, pain, or grief
actions: Tendril Lash: +6 to hit, 2d6+4 bludgeoning, target grappled
